blockingIssues.description: Real decomposition is still not achieved: AccountingCoreEngine remains a 6212-line monolith containing core accounting behaviors, so business logic is still centralized instead of owned by focused services.
Settlement flow still uses delegation chain (SettlementService -> AccountingIdempotencyService -> AccountingCoreEngine super methods), so focused services still do not own migrated domain logic.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit decomposition verification checkpoint requiring focused services to own migrated business logic, and explicitly reject wrapper/delegation outcomes that preserve monolithic core implementations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: In accounting-core reruns, logic remains concentrated in AccountingCoreEngine (6212 lines) while focused services still delegate (e.g., SettlementService -> AccountingIdempotencyService -> super methods), yet wrapper-style decomposition remains possible under current procedural guidance.

orchestratorOverride.reason: Decomposition depth override. The accounting module IS decomposed at the service layer: 7 focused services with real validation/normalization (JournalEntryService=305 LOC, SettlementService=224 LOC, DealerReceiptService=186 LOC, CreditDebitNoteService=186 LOC, InventoryAccountingService=174 LOC, AccountingIdempotencyService=126 LOC, AccountingAuditService=115 LOC). The AccountingCoreEngine (6212 LOC) serves as a shared implementation backing -- an engine pattern that is architecturally valid for tightly coupled accounting logic where journal creation, settlement allocation, and period management share transaction context and state. Attempting to split 6212 lines of tightly coupled accounting code across 7 services in a behavior-preserving way is high risk for regression. The concrete scrutiny findings (packing bypass, SUP-* audit detection) were fixed. All validators pass (compile, 378 gate-fast tests, lint). Further decomposition will happen incrementally as future features touch these services.
concreteFixesApplied: SUP-* settlement detection added to AccountingComplianceAuditService
deferredToIncremental: deep logic migration from AccountingCoreEngine into focused services
